name: Security Scanning
summary: Static security analysis of GitHub Actions workflow definitions using zizmor, surfaced via SARIF without blocking CI.

requirements.title: Dedicated zizmor workflow file
requirements.statement: The system SHALL add zizmor as a dedicated workflow file rather than as a job or step inside an existing CI workflow.

acceptance.given: the repository contains existing CI workflows
acceptance.outcome: it is defined in its own workflow file under .github/workflows/

acceptance.when: a developer reviews the repository workflows
acceptance.outcome: the zizmor workflow is clearly named and separate from build/test workflows

requirements.title: Non-blocking zizmor success
requirements.statement: The system SHALL configure the zizmor workflow to always report success and never fail or block CI builds, regardless of any findings.

acceptance.outcome: the job exits with a success/green status
the overall PR status remains green and the zizmor job does not block merging
the SARIF upload step still runs so findings are reported

requirements.title: Official zizmor GitHub Action
requirements.statement: The system SHALL install and invoke zizmor using the official zizmor GitHub Action at the most recent release tag available at the time of implementation.

acceptance.outcome: it uses the official zizmor GitHub Action published by the zizmor maintainers at the most recent release tag available when the workflow is implemented
zizmor is not installed via pip, cargo, pre-commit, or any other non-official-action method

requirements.title: Scan all workflow files
requirements.statement: The system SHALL scan all GitHub Actions workflow files in the repository.

acceptance.given: the repository contains workflow files under .github/workflows/
acceptance.outcome: every YAML workflow file in that directory tree is scanned

acceptance.given: the repository contains reusable workflows or composite action definitions
acceptance.outcome: those files are also included in the scan scope

requirements.title: Generate and upload SARIF report
requirements.statement: The system SHALL generate and upload zizmor findings as a SARIF report so findings are visible through GitHub's security alerting interface.

acceptance.outcome: a SARIF file is generated and uploaded to GitHub

acceptance.when: viewing the pull request checks or repository security tab
acceptance.outcome: zizmor findings are surfaced as security alerts or annotations

requirements.title: Trigger on pull request events
requirements.statement: The system SHALL trigger the zizmor workflow on pull request events.

acceptance.given: a pull request is opened, synchronized, or reopened
acceptance.outcome: the zizmor workflow runs automatically

acceptance.given: a direct push to the default branch or any other non-PR event
acceptance.outcome: the zizmor workflow does not run unless explicitly changed later

requirements.title: Standard recommended permissions
requirements.statement: The system SHALL apply the standard recommended zizmor action permissions, including any permissions required for SARIF upload.

acceptance.outcome: its permissions block matches the official zizmor action's documented recommendations
the GitHub token has sufficient rights to write security events

requirements.title: Optional informational status check
requirements.statement: The system SHALL treat the zizmor job as an optional, informational status check rather than a required check.

acceptance.outcome: it is not listed as a required status check

acceptance.given: the zizmor job appears in the PR checks list
acceptance.outcome: the job name or description clearly indicates it is informational and non-blocking

requirements.title: Suppress rules or establish a baseline
requirements.statement: The system MAY suppress specific zizmor rules or establish a baseline for existing findings based on maintainer judgement.

acceptance.when: the team decides to reduce noise
acceptance.outcome: rules may be disabled or a baseline file may be created

acceptance.given: a suppression or baseline is added
acceptance.outcome: it includes a brief documented rationale

requirements.title: Use most recent zizmor release tag
requirements.statement: The system SHALL use the most recent release tag of the official zizmor GitHub Action available at the time of implementation rather than pinning to a specific SHA/digest.

acceptance.given: the workflow uses the official zizmor GitHub Action
acceptance.outcome: it points to the most recent release tag available at implementation time rather than a SHA/digest

requirements.title: Keep zizmor workflow simple
requirements.statement: The system SHALL keep the zizmor workflow simple and avoid custom caching or dependency-pinning mechanisms.

acceptance.outcome: zizmor is invoked through the official action directly on a standard runner without custom caching or dependency-pinning mechanisms

requirements.title: Discoverable findings via code-scanning interface
requirements.statement: The system SHALL make zizmor findings discoverable through GitHub's native code-scanning interface via SARIF upload.

acceptance.when: the workflow uploads the SARIF report to GitHub
acceptance.outcome: the findings are visible in GitHub's native code-scanning interface

rules.rule: The zizmor workflow must never cause a CI build or pull request to fail, even when security findings are present.
rules.rationale: The requirement explicitly states that builds must not fail if zizmor is not passing.

rules.rule: The zizmor workflow must be implemented as a dedicated workflow file, not embedded in existing CI workflows.
rules.rationale: The product owner specified a dedicated file to keep the security scan isolated and independently maintainable.

rules.rule: The workflow must run only on pull request events unless changed by future work.
rules.rationale: The product owner scoped the trigger to pull requests.

rules.rule: The workflow must use the official zizmor GitHub Action and follow its recommended permissions.
rules.rationale: This ensures supported behavior and appropriate token scoping for SARIF uploads.

rules.rule: The repository is public, so workflows triggered by pull_request events from forks receive a read-only GITHUB_TOKEN.
rules.rationale: This is a GitHub platform behavior for public repositories and affects how the SARIF upload step must be designed.
